title: Module 4 knowledge check
markdown: How LLMs generate text — and what they do not do.

questions.prompt: A large language model chooses each next word by predicting likely continuations from patterns in its training data, not by looking up verified facts.
A token in an LLM is best described as:
choices: A chunk of text the model processes (often a word piece)
A cryptographic password the model invents

questions.prompt: Which claim is most accurate about LLMs?
choices: They can produce fluent text that is still factually wrong
They always cite primary sources correctly
They replace the need for human review in medicine by default

questions.prompt: When you chat with a typical consumer LLM product, you are usually:
choices: Running inference on a pre-trained model
Retraining the entire model from zero with each message
Performing unsupervised clustering of your ISP

questions.prompt: Example AI outputs shown in educational materials should be treated as possibly wrong illustrations, not guaranteed truth.
Generative models for images and audio:
choices: Can also reflect training-data patterns and biases, and need critical review
